title: Nondisclosure of information in exceptional circumstances
doc_html:
Art. 1303.12. Nondisclosure of information in exceptional circumstances

             If a party alleges in an affidavit or a pleading under oath that the health, safety, or liberty of a party or child would be jeopardized by disclosure of specific identifying information, that information shall be sealed and shall not be disclosed to the other party or the public. After a hearing in which a tribunal takes into consideration the health, safety, or liberty of the party or child, the tribunal may order disclosure of information that the tribunal determines to be in the interest of justice.

            Acts 1995, No. 251, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 1996; Acts 1997, No. 1241, §1, eff. July 15, 1997; Acts 2015, No. 80, §1, eff. July 1, 2015.
doc_text:
Art. 1303.12.  Nondisclosure of information in exceptional circumstances
If a party alleges in an affidavit or a pleading under oath that the health, safety, or
liberty of a party or child would be jeopardized by disclosure of specific identifying
information, that information shall be sealed and shall not be disclosed to the other party or
the public.  After a hearing in which a tribunal takes into consideration the health, safety, or
liberty of the party or child, the tribunal may order disclosure of information that the tribunal
determines to be in the interest of justice.
Acts 1995, No. 251, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 1996; Acts 1997, No. 1241, §1, eff. July 15, 1997;
Acts 2015, No. 80, §1, eff. July 1, 2015.
